steps.title: Identify the subject and the required verb form
steps.content:
The subject is Valerie Hollister—a singular proper noun. The intervening appositive phrase (an American abstract painter whose works are distinctive for their stripes and washes) does not change the subject. The blank must be a singular present-tense form of be to complete the passive construction ______ frequently included.
  • Choice A — ‘is’: Singular present-tense form of be; agrees with the singular subject Valerie Hollister. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘are’: Plural present-tense form; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘were’: Plural past-tense form; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘have been’: Plural present-perfect form; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗

steps.title: Confirm subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb. Is is the only option that is both singular and consistent with the present-tense framing of the sentence. That confirms A is correct.
